title: Internals: Improve coverage flow
body:
Please see the added internals doc section on usage, but the high-level usage is:

```
./configure --enable-coverage
.. run whatever you want ..
make coverage-open
```

`make test` takes about 70 minutes on my system (~3x normal clean cache `make test`).
`make coverage-combine` (running fastcov) is ~90 seconds
`make coverage-report` (running genhtml) is ~30 seconds

You can see a codecov.io report here:
- https://app.codecov.io/gh/gezalore/verilator/tree/cover

Highlights:
- We no longer replace the opt build with the dbg build, we just build both with -O0 instead, compile time is not a big issue here, and end-to-end this is marginally faster than using the debug build all the time
- We disable optimization of the runtime lib as well, which we didn't before, coverage now looks much better
- Unused inline functions in headers now show as holes (most of the time)
- We now filter exclusions using fastcov, rather than the previous dance with a cloned source tree. This works well.
- We now upload the exclusion filtered report to codedcov.io (as opposed to the unfiltered one which we did before)
- Uploading to codecov.io no longer runs gcov agan on everything (--plugin noop), but just uses the lcov info file from fastcov (as was expected)
- Full dependency tracking of everything involved in coverage. If you run more test, just run `make coverage*` for the lastest
- CI now uses the reusable workflows for build and test
- Overall CI CPU time is ~20% less, latency is a few minutes shorter.
